title: Apollo Gate 1 -- SPX Structure
macro_explanation: Minor macro setup for 2026-04-15 based on 8 meaningful US event(s): Fed Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman speaks, Fed Beige Book, Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack TV appearance. Current-day US events reviewed: 7.

macro_events.title: Fed Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman speaks
Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack TV appearance
Import price index minus fuel March

macro_minor_events.title: Fed Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman speaks
Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack TV appearance
Import price index minus fuel March

next_market_day_note: Apollo considered 2026-04-15 and confirmed it as the next tradable market day.

structure_attempted_sources.reason: Only 2 usable 5-minute candles were returned for SPX on 2026-04-14, which is insufficient for structure grading.
Retrieved successfully from prior trading session 2026-04-13.

structure_rsi_note: Daily RSI 63.46 does not trigger a structure upgrade.
structure_summary: Structure graded Good: market remained bullish and stable, with trend support intact and no meaningful structural damage.
structure_session_note: Using prior trading session: Mon Apr 13, 2026 (no valid intraday session returned today).

structure_rules.label: Price is mostly above both EMAs
structure_rules.detail: 100.0% of the recent window closed above both EMAs.

structure_rules.label: Price is mostly below both EMAs
structure_rules.detail: Only 0.0% of the recent window closed below both EMAs.

structure_rules.label: EMAs are flat/crossing or price is flipping around both
structure_rules.detail: Recent price action was directional enough that chop was not the dominant classification.

structure_rules.label: No sharp selloff is visible in the session
structure_rules.detail: Max pullback from the session high measured 14.65 points, which is controlled.

structure_rules.label: Recent lows have stabilized instead of continuing lower
structure_rules.detail: Recent lows stabilized rather than extending to fresh local lows.

structure_rules.label: Sustained lower lows and downward continuation are present
structure_rules.detail: Recent price action does not show sustained lower lows and continued downward movement.

structure_recent_price_action: Last 6 completed candles span 6860.65 to 6882.65 with the most recent completed close at 6881.16.
trade_candidates_message: Apollo produced three Gate 3 mode outputs with distinct risk philosophies. No trade execution is performed.

trade_candidates_diagnostics.recommended_contract_size_framework: Standard mode stays at 8 unless macro or final structure = Poor keeps size at 5 because distance is below 2x expected move. Aggressive stays 8 by default and can scale to 10. Fortress stays 8 only with Good final structure and no macro; otherwise 5.

top_rejections.label: Fortress (Max Safety): Rejected: exceeds $15,000 max loss cap
Standard (Apollo Core): Failed 1.80x expected move distance rule
Aggressive (Yield Mode): Failed 1.50x expected move distance rule
Fortress (Max Safety): Failed 2.08x expected move distance rule

mode_summaries.label: Standard (Apollo Core)
mode_summaries.rule_set: Strict 1.80x EM with a fallback to 1.60x EM, using the shared Apollo minimum risk efficiency gate

mode_summaries.label: Aggressive (Yield Mode)
mode_summaries.rule_set: Strict 1.50x EM with the shared Apollo minimum risk efficiency gate and no fallback relaxation

mode_summaries.label: Fortress (Max Safety)
mode_summaries.rule_set: Deterministic Fortress engine: first qualifying short at Standard EM x 1.10 with macro / structure add-ons (1.98x EM fallback), base 15-point width with 10 contracts at short bid / long ask, then neighborhood optimization by credit efficiency

trade_candidates_credit_map.positioning_note: Put-side only positioning shifts SPX right to reveal more downside spread spacing.

trade_candidates_items.mode_descriptor: Balanced risk using Apollo core rules
trade_candidates_items.loss_model_source: Historical real-trade ROI averages only (ordinary losses exclude Black Swans)
trade_candidates_items.recommended_contract_size_reason: Standard mode keeps base size at 8, but macro or final structure = Poor reduces size to 5 until distance reaches 2x expected move.
rationale: Standard (Apollo Core) uses Strict 1.80x EM with a fallback to 1.60x EM, using the shared Apollo minimum risk efficiency gate.
The 6,830/6,805 spread sits 83.90 points from spot, or 1.89x expected move.
Net credit is about 1.25 on 25 points of width.
Standard mode keeps base size at 8, but macro or final structure = Poor reduces size to 5 until distance reaches 2x expected move.
Standard mode balances Apollo's core delta preference with premium only after the full restriction stack passes.
exit_plan: If SPX moves within 30 points of the short strike at 6,830, close 25%.
If SPX moves within 15 points of the short strike at 6,830, close another 25%.
If two consecutive 5-minute candles close fully beyond the short strike at 6,830, close another 25%.
If the long strike at 6,805 is touched, close the remaining 25%.
diagnostics: Rule set used: Strict Pass using 1.80x EM.
Distance vs rule: 83.90 points vs EM threshold 79.74 (Pass).
Distance gate: 1.80x expected move | binding source EM Floor.
Distance vs expected move: 1.89x expected move.
Pricing basis: Hybrid max(executable, midpoint) | short bid 3.70 - long ask 2.55 = 1.15; midpoint model = 1.25; selected net credit = 1.25.
Premium / contract: $125 | Total premium: $625.
Credit efficiency: 5.3% using total premium / total max loss.
Delta compliance: short delta 0.11 | preferred <= 0.12 = Yes | max <= 0.15 = Yes.
Premium received: $625 total | Max loss: $11,875 total.
Black Swan Loss: $7,125 using 60% of theoretical max loss for gap risk and real-world exit behavior.
Contract sizing decision: 5 contracts | Standard mode keeps base size at 8, but macro or final structure = Poor reduces size to 5 until distance reaches 2x expected move.
Risk cap allowed: $20,250 | status: Within risk cap.
Original size 5 -> adjusted size 5 | 8.80% of account at risk.
Exit ladder applied: Yes.
Break-even buffer vs lower expected-move boundary: 40.85 points.
Other candidates rejected for: Risk efficiency below 0.05 (1125); Failed 1.80x expected move distance rule (128); Net credit below 1.00 (7).

trade_candidates_items.mode_descriptor: Higher premium with controlled risk relaxation
trade_candidates_items.loss_model_source: Historical real-trade ROI averages only (ordinary losses exclude Black Swans)
trade_candidates_items.recommended_contract_size_reason: Aggressive mode keeps the default 8-contract size because scale-up conditions were not met.
rationale: Aggressive (Yield Mode) uses Strict 1.50x EM with the shared Apollo minimum risk efficiency gate and no fallback relaxation.
The 6,845/6,810 spread sits 68.90 points from spot, or 1.56x expected move.
Net credit is about 2.20 on 35 points of width.
Aggressive mode keeps the default 8-contract size because scale-up conditions were not met.
Aggressive mode optimizes premium once the 1.5% SPX rule and 1.0x expected-move safety floor are satisfied.
exit_plan: If SPX moves within 30 points of the short strike at 6,845, close 25%.
If SPX moves within 15 points of the short strike at 6,845, close another 25%.
If two consecutive 5-minute candles close fully beyond the short strike at 6,845, close another 25%.
If the long strike at 6,810 is touched, close the remaining 25%.
diagnostics: Rule set used: Strict Pass using 1.50x EM.
Distance vs rule: 68.90 points vs EM threshold 66.45 (Pass).
Distance gate: 1.50x expected move | binding source EM Floor.
Distance vs expected move: 1.56x expected move.
Pricing basis: Hybrid max(executable, midpoint) | short bid 4.80 - long ask 2.75 = 2.05; midpoint model = 2.20; selected net credit = 2.20.
Premium / contract: $220 | Total premium: $1,760.
Credit efficiency: 6.7% using total premium / total max loss.
Delta compliance: short delta 0.15 | preferred <= 0.20 = Yes | max <= 0.25 = Yes.
Premium received: $1,760 total | Max loss: $26,240 total.
Black Swan Loss: $15,744 using 60% of theoretical max loss for gap risk and real-world exit behavior.
Contract sizing decision: 8 contracts | Aggressive mode keeps the default 8-contract size because scale-up conditions were not met.
Risk cap allowed: $27,000 | status: Within risk cap.
Original size 8 -> adjusted size 8 | 19.44% of account at risk.
Exit ladder applied: Yes.
Break-even buffer vs lower expected-move boundary: 26.80 points.
Other candidates rejected for: Risk efficiency below 0.05 (1125); Failed 1.50x expected move distance rule (104); Net credit below 1.00 (14); Credit-to-width ratio too small (1).

trade_candidates_items.mode_descriptor: Maximum distance and lowest probability of touch
trade_candidates_items.loss_model_source: Historical real-trade ROI averages only (ordinary losses exclude Black Swans)
trade_candidates_items.recommended_contract_size_reason: Fortress optimizer selected this contract size using the credit-efficiency priority stack.
rationale: Fortress must clear 2.08x expected move before any optimization is considered.
The selected short strike is 6,820, 93.90 points from spot at 2.12x expected move.
Selection priority is credit efficiency first, then highest total premium, then lowest max loss.
exit_plan: If SPX moves within 30 points of the short strike at 6,820, close 25%.
If SPX moves within 15 points of the short strike at 6,820, close another 25%.
If two consecutive 5-minute candles close fully beyond the short strike at 6,820, close another 25%.
If the long strike at 6,800 is touched, close the remaining 25%.
diagnostics: Optimized Fortress candidate replaced the deterministic base candidate.
Candidate evaluated inside the Fortress neighborhood optimizer.
Fortress pricing basis: Short = Bid, Long = Ask.
Base EM rule used: 2.08x expected move.
Net credit / contract: $0.75 using short bid 3.10 and long ask 2.35.
Total premium received: $525 across 7 contracts.
Max theoretical loss: $13,475 total | $1,925 per contract.
Credit efficiency: 3.9% = premium received / max theoretical loss.
Break-even buffer vs lower expected move boundary: 50.42 points.
